sentence: Here is the SOAP note based on the provided transcript:
Subjective:
HPI: The patient, Gregory, is a 2-month-old nonhealing foot ulcer on the dorsal aspect of the lateral right foot, which has recently become red and is draining.
He reports that the wound throbs occasionally, but he does not have specific pain.
His biggest concern is the redness and drainage.
He mentions that he got the wound from walking barefoot on the beach at Dewey Beach.
Past medical history: The patient has diabetes, for which he takes medication but does not regularly check his blood sugar levels.
His last A1c is around 10%, which is considered high.
He has not seen his PCP in a while and has not filled his prescribed antibiotics.
Review of systems: The patient reports occasional throbbing pain in the affected foot.
Current medications: [NOT MENTIONED]
Objective:
Vital signs: The patient's vital signs are normal, with no fever.
Physical exam: The physical exam reveals a 1x2 inch circular wound on the dorsal aspect of the lateral right foot, proximal to the fifth MTP joint, with redness, drainage, edema, and fluid present.
The patient reports increased pressure when the wound is touched, but no distinct pain.
Test Results: An X-ray of the right foot shows no evidence of osteomyelitis.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has a diabetic foot ulcer with a suspected infection.
Problem list: Diabetic foot ulcer; Suspected infection; Uncontrolled diabetes; Poor wound care and lack of follow-up with PCP.
Plan:
I prescribed clindamycin 400mg twice daily for 7 days to treat the suspected infection.
I will perform a debridement to remove dead tissue from the wound.
I will also order an ABI to determine the blood supply to the foot.
The patient will need to wear a surgical shoe to reduce pressure on the affected foot.
I will see the patient back in 2 weeks for follow-up and possible repeat debridement.
Follow-up: The patient will be seen back in 2 weeks for follow-up and possible repeat debridement.
